summary: Backend (12/12 pytest) and Frontend e2e tested for the Wedding Invite MVP. Full flow works: Home -> Admin (/guests) -> create guest -> visit /invite/<slug> -> envelope shows guest name -> click wax seal -> envelope opens (~2.1s) -> invite page with countdown, story, schedule, venue, gallery, signoff. Hashtag #AnanyaForArjun, date Dec 14 2026, venues in Udaipur all rendered. No critical issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py @app.on_event('shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI - migrate to lifespan handler.
Wedding info is auto-seeded on first GET /api/wedding which mutates DB on a read; consider explicit seed script or upsert with default values to avoid a side-effect in a GET.

InvitePage falls back to constructing a 'name' from slug when guest is unknown rather than showing a 404/empty state — this means /invite/anything renders an invite. Acceptable for the demo route /invite/our-dearest-friend used on the homepage, but worth noting for security/UX.
GuestsPage onDelete has no error handling (try/catch missing); silent failure if backend errors.
No data-testid on the textarea label/form container; minor.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Test guests prefixed with 'TEST' are created during pytest and cleaned up via teardown_module. Wedding info auto-seeds on first /api/wedding call.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/wedding_invite_test.py cover wedding info, guest CRUD, slug generation, duplicate handling, and 400/404 paths. Frontend uses data-testid: demo-invite-link, admin-link, admin-guest-list, add-guest-input, add-guest-submit, guest-row-{slug}, copy-link-button-{slug}, preview-link-{slug}, delete-guest-{slug}, guest-name-display, open-envelope-button, invite-page, countdown-timer. Envelope animation ~2.1s. When checking text inside the invite, remember CSS uppercase changes the rendered string (innerText returns uppercase).
